item.text: I’m tracing the audit wiring directly in the workspace and will verify both parts from code rather than memory. I’ll search for `PolicyDenial` construction sites and then locate every `AuditSink` impl with exact file paths.
I’ve got the candidate files. Next I’m reading the exact construction and `impl` sites so the final list only includes files that actually synthesize the event, not ones that merely match on it.

One of the initial hits in the smoke example is only a match arm, not a constructor, so I’m broadening the search pattern to catch any other real constructors and to avoid false positives from enum definitions or readers.

## A. PolicyDenial constructors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs`: `AgentLoop` emits `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial` when a requested tool is rejected by the loop allowlist before dispatch (`sink.emit(&LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... })` at line 273).
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink` synthesizes a new `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial` when it sees a disallowed `ToolCallRequested` and mirrors that denial into the inner sink (`let denial = LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` at line 92).

## B. AuditSink implementors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `NullSink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `InMemorySink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `JsonlFileSink`
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink`
